args.text: Chapter 3: The Collapse of the Fringe

display.center:
Target: Four Unregistered Persons
All Registered Residents are obligated to report.

events.text: Their faces appeared on the broadcast screen just as {{characters.lyra.name}} was biting into a compressed ration bar.
She looked up at the corroded announcement display on the wall. The image quality was poor, extracted from surveillance cameras, but the silhouettes were clear enough—all four of them.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Maximum bounty rating. All registered residents are obligated to report, with a reward equal to three months' standard rations.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Three months. That's incentive enough to turn us in.

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: The black market. Black market people won't report us because they're on wanted lists too. Mutual betrayal would destroy everyone.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Correct. Plus the black market has information we need—which routes are still clear, which checkpoints can be bribed, where we can find temporary shelter.
He set down his pen and looked at the others.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Complete intelligence gathering by sunset, change locations by dawn. This base is no longer safe.
The problem was that the black market lay deep in the lower decks. They had to cross two main thoroughfares to get there, and those thoroughfares were now far more heavily patrolled than usual.

{{characters.soren.name}} divided the route into three segments, with a rally point designated for each. If anyone got separated mid-transit, they'd wait at that point for a set duration. Beyond that window, they'd scatter and regroup later.
{{characters.lyra.name}} finished the last of her ration bar, brushed crumbs from her hands, and stood. She took a few steps toward the door, then looked back at the other three.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Everyone looks like we're marching toward our deaths. <delay ms="400"/>Relax a little. We're just getting started.
No one laughed. They moved out.

Crossing the first segment, {{characters.soren.name}}'s timing was so precise that {{characters.elias.name}} noticed. Patrol footsteps faded from a corner just as they crossed an intersection, slipping into the next corridor against the wall with barely a pause between movements.

The second segment presented a temporary checkpoint that {{characters.soren.name}} detected two blocks early. He diverted, trading some distance for safety, timing the delay within acceptable parameters.
The black market was hidden in the oldest cluster of structures on the lower decks. Its entrance was an iron door that appeared welded shut.
Three knocks on a specific spot on the frame would bring someone from inside to open it.
